Title: Counting a billion people: Inside India's mega census drive
Article:
SUKHWINDER Kaur battles the scorching sun as she trudges door-to-door in New Delhi as part of India's colossal exercise to count more than one billion citizens.
Kaur is among three million census enumerators fanning out across cities and villages in an exercise that will help shape policy for the country's estimated 1.4 billion people for years to come.
Billed as the world's largest population count, the census is being held in two phases at a cost of US$1.25 billion, according to the government.
Last week, the heat became overwhelming for Kaur, a government school teacher.
"I went back home, but returned again in the evening," said Kaur, who will be paid 25,000 rupees (US$270) for the assignment, like other census enumerators.
Feebly knocking on a door in the servants' quarters of an upscale housing society, Kaur, 38, tried to explain the purpose of her visit to an elderly resident.
"Because we have been handling children, we have, in a way, learned how to deal with all kinds of people," she said.
"Some speak politely, some behave strangely but we know how to handle them."
In the first phase, census takers ask 33 questions related to water, sanitation, electricity, cooking fuel and internet.
The second phase next year will focus on economic status as well as the more contentious question of caste – the millennia-old social hierarchy that is deeply entwined with Indian politics and welfare entitlements.
As in the past, census results will be released in phases starting late next year.
The Hindu-majority nation faces mounting challenges in providing housing, electricity and other basic needs to its growing population.
Many of its megacities are already grappling with water shortages and overcrowded slums.
[[nid:1423936]]
The government says the first phase "forms an essential foundation for evidence-based planning and formulation of welfare and development programmes."
In the southern tech hub of Bengaluru, teacher Sheikh Shavali held on to his cap to shield against the sun as he made his way through the cramped lanes of Maya Bazaar.
"I take a break once every two hours,"  he said with a smile.
Enumerators have to survey 20-25 houses a day but Shavali, 50, has no complaints.
"I am treating this work as my small contribution to help these people. If I get the data out correctly, proper schemes can be made for them."
Most of the houses in the area, dotted with open drains, do not have a toilet. Residents have to walk several meters to use the nearest public lavatory.
But Shavali noted most of the households had at least one smartphone, underscoring India's uneven growth.
For the first time, the census is being conducted through a dedicated mobile app.
But the digital shift has not been seamless.
Lalit Verma, a 48-year-old Delhi teacher, said he was also taking notes in a diary in case the data is lost due to glitches.
Others have welcomed the change. Bengaluru teacher Anandi A. said the app was "easy to use."
"It takes about five to 10 minutes to finish putting in details of one household," she said.
Punith, who uses one name and has been tasked with training census takers in Bengaluru, said earlier it would take "many months" for data to be scrutinised and verified.
[[nid:1423935]]
"Now (within) three to four hours, I will sync (the data) and it goes to supervisor, then the supervisor will approve it and the census gets completed," he told AFP.
India's last census was held in 2011. The one planned in 2021 was delayed by the Covid-19 pandemic.
The groundwork for the latest census included training sessions that focus as much on temperament as on technology.
At one such session attended by AFP in Bengaluru, enumerators were instructed on how first impressions could make or break an interview.
"Greet politely, state your name and purpose, and immediately show your official ID," the guidelines read.
Enumerators must dress neatly and speak calmly, avoiding any tone that might sound hurried or rude.
They are also trained to explain to residents that their personal data is protected under the Census Act.
Refusals are expected, and trainees are advised to offer return visits rather than push for answers.
Back in Delhi, Verma encountered locked homes, hesitant residents and houses where only children were present.
"I have noted the house numbers," he said.
"I will revisit those homes where complete information was not received."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 146 held in Terengganu anti-drug crackdown
Article:
CHUKAI: The National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) detained 146 individuals, including four women, in a five-day operation carried out across Terengganu from Saturday.
State AADK director Arzmi Abdullah said the arrests were made in Kemaman, Setiu and Marang, involving individuals aged between 18 and 61.
"All were detained under Section 3(1) of the Drug Dependants (Treatment and Rehabilitation) Act 1983 after testing positive for drugs," he said at a press conference in Seri Bandi near here today.
Of the total, 132 tested positive for methamphetamine, while seven each tested positive for heroin and ketum.
Those detained comprised 142 men and four women, including civil servants, labourers, restaurant workers and a single mother.
Arzmi said six officers and 69 personnel were involved in the operation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PetChem expects continued market uncertainty, supply chain volatility in 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) is heading into 2026 expecting continued market uncertainty and supply chain volatility, driven in part by uneven global recovery and recent geopolitical developments in Middle East affecting feedstock flows.
PetChem said it will focus on operational discipline and tighter capital management as it navigates a more challenging environment, while selectively advancing initiatives to strengthen portfolio resilience.
This includes optimising its core business, expanding into specialty chemicals in a measured way, and making targeted investments aligned with evolving customer demand.
Managing director and chief executive officer Mazuin Ismail said the group's priority for 2026 is to maintain vigilance, reinforce discipline and safeguard the strength of its operations, while continuing to build the foundations for sustainable growth.
"In an operating environment that has become more dynamic and less predictable, this approach ensures we remain resilient through near-term volatility and well-positioned to emerge stronger.
"We remain committed to prioritising domestic demand over exports to ensure the continued availability of high-quality products in the local market.
"Customers can rely on us for stable supply and consistent product quality," he said in a statement following PetChem's 28th annual general meeting (AGM) today.
At the AGM, PetChem said its financial and operational performance for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 reflected disciplined execution amid global headwinds in the chemicals market.
Against a backdrop of oversupply, weak demand, cost pressures and geopolitical uncertainty, the group recorded a plant utilisation rate of 88 per cent, supported by strong asset integrity and improved plant management across its portfolio.
PetChem also posted a RM574 million improvement in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation, despite weaker pricing conditions, driven by feedstock optimisation, better plant reliability and more efficient turnaround management.
It said commercial agility, including strategic third-party sourcing and active market optimisation, improved supply flexibility, while structural cost savings were achieved through tighter contract management, logistics efficiency and waste circularity initiatives.
Reflecting its performance, the group announced a total dividend of RM560 million for financial year 2025, balancing shareholder returns with financial flexibility for future growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLIA baggage disruption sparks transport security review, weekly reports ordered - Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A five-hour disruption to the baggage handling system at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) caused by a power outage has prompted tighter oversight on transport operational security, with Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) ordered to submit weekly progress reports to the Transport Ministry.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the incident, which affected baggage services at KLIA, lasted about five hours before being fully restored at 10.30pm.
"The disruption was due to a power supply interruption, and operations were restored at 10.30pm," he said at the post-Cabinet weekly briefing today.
Fahmi said MAHB will implement immediate mitigation measures, including increasing manpower at arrival halls and other identified critical areas to reduce the risk of similar operational breakdowns.
He added that the Transport Ministry will receive weekly updates on corrective actions and improvements being carried out by MAHB.
"The MAHB will implement a number of immediate steps, including increasing the number of staff at arrival areas and other critical locations identified," he said.
The incident has raised concerns over operational resilience at Malaysia's main international gateway, particularly in ensuring continuity of essential airport systems during infrastructure failures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Global energy crisis may normalise faster than Covid-19 impact: Affin chief economist
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The global energy shock triggered by the Middle East conflict is expected to normalise faster than the prolonged economic downturn seen during the Covid-19 pandemic.
However, Affin Bank chief economist Alan Tan Chew Leong said the outlook remains highly dependent on how long the conflict persists.
"During Covid-19, the shock in the oil supply took about 18 months to go back to normalcy. However this time, if the war ends and the Strait of Hormuz is open for all tankers, we think the oil disruption is likely to take a shorter period to go back to normalcy."
He said this in a webinar hosted by the Economic Club of Kuala Lumpur and Asean Economic Club on the Middle East conflict today.
Domestically, Tan said demand in the country is strong with growth coming from private consumption and investment. However, inflation remains as a downside risk to growth which is going to eat into disposable income.
Another concern from the energy shock is the amount of oil supply that the country currently has.
"How long will the supply last? Is it sufficient until June and when would we get the next shipment? This remains uncertain," said Tan.
He added that the government's newly-announced assistance for micro, small and medium enterprises will help to cushion the rising cost and its impact on the economy.
"Assuming the economy shows signs of slowing down, the government would come out with some fiscal stimulus from cash assistance like Sumbangan Asas Rahmah. This would provide some cushion to help in terms of supporting consumption spending," he said.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on Tuesday announced that the government would add another RM5 billion in special relief fund to support MSMEs affected by the global energy crisis.
The additional support comes after the Finance Ministry announced a series of measures to help MSMEs, which include a RM5 billion allocation under Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan on Monday.
IPP Global Wealth director, investment strategist and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said Malaysia is still on track to achieve the higher end of the gross domestic product range at 4.5 per cent.
However, he said the sentiment on the global equity market remains fragile even as tensions in the Middle East subsides mainly due to the absence of a clear political settlement.
Regionally, Sedek said the broader inflation dynamic will be uneven and investors will adjust their expectations for corporates in light of the current situation.
Malaysia's headline inflation rose to 1.7 per cent year-on-year in March 2026, driven by rising fuel prices and supply-side pressures. This marks an increase from 1.4 per cent in February but remains within a manageable range.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senior citizen, Indonesian single mother fined for wrongful confinement of migrants
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A senior citizen and an Indonesian single mother were fined RM3,000 each by the magistrate's court here today for the wrongful confinement of two migrants and the illegal possession of their passports.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass handed down the sentence after Ker Peng Hoe, 71, and Hartati Agostoni, 43, pleaded guilty to the offences.
The duo were charged under Section 340 of the Penal Code for wrongful confinement, punishable under Section 342 and read together with Section 34 of the same Code for common intention.
They were charged with wrongfully confining two Indonesian women at a shophouse in Taman Flora Utama between 9am and 8.30pm on April 19.
For the first charge, the court fined the duo RM2,000 each.
Under the second charge, both were accused of being in illegal possession of the victims' passports at the Batu Pahat police headquarters and in a room in Taman Flora Utama between 3.30pm and 4.30pm on April 20.
The second charge was framed under Section 12(1)(f) of the Passports Act 1966, which carries a maximum fine of RM10,000, up to five years' imprisonment, or both. The court fined them RM1,000 each for this offence.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while lawyers Tan Song Yan and Tan Fong Ling represented the accused.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian woman cries for her boyfriend after being charged with prostitution
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An Indonesian woman broke down in tears while searching for her boyfriend after being charged in the magistrate's court today with prostitution and abusing her social visit pass.
Waryanti, 41, pleaded not guilty to the charges before magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
Her compatriot, Wayan Lawungwati, 38, who was charged separately, pleaded guilty.
As she was led away after the proceedings, a distraught Waryanti was heard crying out, "Where is my boyfriend?"
Earlier, both women were accused of soliciting for prostitution at a shophouse in Taman Maju Bistari, Parit Raja, at 6pm on April 9.
The offence, framed under Section 372B of the Penal Code, carries a penalty of up to one year in jail, a fine, or both.
For the second charge, they were accused of violating the conditions of their social visit passes by engaging in prostitution at the same time and location.
This charge was framed under Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan handled the prosecution, while the accused were represented by Mohd Razak Shariff.
The court sentenced Wayan Lawungwati to 14 days' jail and a RM4,000 fine for the first charge, and a RM1,000 fine for the second.
The magistrate ordered the accused to serve an additional nine months in prison if she failed to pay the fines. She was later sent to Kluang Prison after failing to settle the amount.
For Waryanti, the court fixed May 22 for mention. No bail was offered as she is a foreign national.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC opens 65 investigations over 463 fake news takedown requests - Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has received 463 requests to remove online content linked to fake news surrounding global supply chain issues, with authorities opening 65 investigation papers.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cases primarily involved misinformation related to fuel prices and developments surrounding the Strait of Hormuz.
He said the highest volume of fake news content was detected on social media platforms, including Facebook, TikTok and Threads.
"Total applications received were 463 as of 7am today," he said at the post-Cabinet briefing.
He said of the total cases, enforcement action has already been initiated.
"Out of these 463 applications, 65 investigation papers have been opened," he said.
Fahmi said one case has already been decided to be brought to court, while five others have received advice from the Attorney-General's Chambers for compounds.
"The remaining cases are still under investigation," he said.
He added that the government was concerned about the spread of misleading information during a period of heightened global economic uncertainty.
According to him, misinformation was most commonly linked to fuel pricing and claims related to the Strait of Hormuz, which has been a focal point of global supply chain concerns.
The government has repeatedly cautioned against the spread of unverified content, particularly on issues involving national economic stability and essential goods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chief Secretary tasked with studying private sector WFH savings to assess national impact
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar has been tasked with gathering data from the private sector on work-from-home (WFH) practices to assess the broader national impact of remote working, including potential fuel and operational cost savings.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the move is part of efforts to obtain a more comprehensive picture of savings achieved beyond the public sector, which has already recorded significant reductions in fuel usage.
He said the government had analysed savings generated through civil service WFH arrangements and wants to study the assessment on private sector participation.
"The Chief Secretary has also been reminded to obtain information from the private sector on the implementation of work-from-home arrangements in that sector, so that we can look at overall savings," he said at the post-Cabinet briefing today.
The directive comes as the public sector has recorded fuel savings of 334,000 litres, worth RM678,000, since mid-April through expanded remote working arrangements.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six bands advance to RTD 'Battle of the Bands' finals
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Six bands from various higher education institutions have secured their spots in the grand finals of the "Battle of the Bands" competition, an innovative road safety advocacy initiative by the Road Transport Department (RTD).
The finals are set to take place about 9pm this Friday at Batu Kawan Stadium in Penang.
Organised by the RTD, the programme is designed to use music as a creative medium to deliver road safety messages to the public.
The competition is held in conjunction with RTD's 80th anniversary celebrations and the JPJ Madani Mega Carnival.
In a statement today, Selangor RTD director Azrin Borhan said the competition aims to adopt a more relaxed and approachable method of reaching all levels of society.
"The goal is to increase public awareness of the importance of road safety and to encourage a shift towards more cautious and responsible driving habits," the statement read.
The six finalists were selected following a rigorous screening process by a panel of professional judges.
Evaluations were based on creativity, performance quality, originality, and the participants' ability to effectively incorporate high-impact road safety messages into their sets.
The six finalists vying for the top prize are Nada Stereo from Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris; Tralala from Universiti Malaya; Scarla from Universiti Utara Malaysia; Putra Simponi from Universiti Putra Malaysia; Difficulties from Akademi Seni Budaya dan Warisan Kebangsaan; and Minit Terakhir from Universiti Sains Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Extreme heat threatens global food systems, UN agencies warn
Article:
ROME: Extreme heat is pushing global agrifood systems to the brink, threatening the livelihoods and health of more than a billion people, according to a new report by the UN's food and weather agencies.
The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) and the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) said heatwaves are becoming more frequent, intense and prolonged, damaging crops, livestock, fisheries and forests.
"Extreme heat is rewriting the script on what farmers, fishers and foresters can grow and when they can grow. In some cases it is even dictating if they can still work," said Kaveh Zahedi, head of FAO's climate change office.
"At its core, this report is telling us that we face a very uncertain future," he told Reuters.
Recent climate datasets show global warming is accelerating, with 2025 ranking among the three hottest years on record, triggering more frequent and severe weather extremes.
Acting as a risk multiplier, extreme heat intensifies droughts, wildfires and pest outbreaks and sharply cuts crop yields once critical temperature thresholds are breached.
The report said higher temperatures are shrinking the safety margin that plants, animals and humans rely on to function, with yields for most major crops falling once temperatures exceed about 30 degrees Celsius (86 degrees Fahrenheit).
Zahedi cited Morocco, where six years of drought were followed by record heatwaves. "This led to a fall in cereal yields by over 40 per cent. It decimated the olive and citrus harvest. Basically, those harvests failed," he said.
Marine heatwaves are also becoming more frequent, depleting oxygen levels in water and threatening fish stocks. In 2024, 91 per cent of the world's oceans experienced at least one marine heatwave, the report said.
Risks rise sharply as warming accelerates. The intensity of extreme heat events is expected to roughly double at two degrees Celsius of warming and quadruple at three degrees, compared with 1.5 degrees, the report said.
Zahedi said every one-degree rise in average global temperatures cuts yields of the world's four major crops - maize, rice, soya, and wheat - by about 6 per cent.
The FAO and WMO said piecemeal responses were inadequate and called for better risk governance and early-warning weather systems to help farmers and fishers take preventive action.
"If you can get the data into the farmers' hands, they can adjust when they plant, they can adjust what they plant, they can adjust when they harvest," Zahedi said.
But the report said adaptation alone is not enough, arguing the only lasting solution to the growing threat of extreme heat is ambitious, coordinated action to curb climate change.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia pivots to asymmetric warfare with focus on missiles, drones
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is recalibrating its defence strategy towards asymmetric warfare, with a stronger emphasis on missiles, drones and network-centric systems to address evolving security challenges.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said recent procurements have focused on enhancing naval combat capabilities, particularly for assets operating in strategic waters.
He said the acquisitions include surface-to-surface and surface-to-air missile systems for Littoral Combat Ships (LCS) and Littoral Mission Ships (LMS), aimed at strengthening operational readiness in key areas such as the Straits of Malacca, South China Sea, Sulu Sea and Sulawesi Sea.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tuanku Muhriz to officiate Negri Sembilan assembly sitting tomorrow
Article:
SEREMBAN: Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, is scheduled to officiate the opening of the first meeting of the fourth session of the 15th State Legislative Assembly tomorrow.
State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim said the ruler is expected to deliver the opening address at 10am after inspecting a guard of honour.
Following tomorrow's ceremony, the assembly sitting will continue from April 27 to 29.
"The opening ceremony will proceed as scheduled. Tuanku Muhriz will be present to officiate. Regarding VVIP invitations, I cannot elaborate, but the ruler will be there," he told reporters at Wisma Negeri today.
He was asked if preparations for the assembly sitting had been disrupted following an ongoing issue involving the Undang Yang Empat and the Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
Zafir could not confirm the attendance of other dignitaries, including the Undang Yang Empat and the Tunku Besar Tampin, although official invitations had been sent.
Customarily, the ceremony is attended by the four Undang: Datuk Mubarak Dohak (Sungei Ujong), Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad (Jelebu), Datuk Muhammad Abdullah (Johol), and Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin (Rembau), as well as Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al Qadri.
Under the official protocol, Tunku Syed Razman is traditionally the first to enter the assembly hall, followed by the four Undang.
A rehearsal for the guard of honour, comprising three officers and 103 personnel from the 14th Battalion of the Royal Malay Regiment, was held ahead of the opening.
The state has recently drawn attention following reports of a constitutional dispute.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perlis mufti denies links to misappropriation of zakat funds
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Perlis Mufti Datuk Dr Mohd Asri Zainul Abidin has denied any links between his office and the
alleged misappropriation of RM230 million
in zakat funds.
Asri said zakat administration in the state is managed by the Perlis Islamic Religious and Malay Customs Council (MAIPs) rather than the mufti's office.
"The mufti's role is limited to issuing fatwas on what is permissible. I do not manage or distribute zakat funds, nor do I have the authority to issue a zakat receipt," he said in a video posted on his Facebook page.
Asri said zakat contributors in Perlis can propose recipients, such as non-governmental organisations (NGOs) or religious institutions.
"MAIPs will verify whether these proposed recipients qualify under asnaf categories before allocating about 50 to 60 per cent of the contribution to them. The funds go directly to the recipient," he said.
He said that a certain NGO with a wide network often reaches out to contributors and proposes that donations be made to them via MAIPs.
"Because they approach the masses, the amount collected is huge," he said, adding that the NGO carries out charity work. He did not name the NGO.
He said while MAIPs request expenditure reports from such NGOs, it does not have the power to conduct independent audits on them.
"The NGO performs its own audit and provides a report to MAIPs. MAIPs itself has been audited for years without any reprimands," he said.
On the claims of misappropriation, Asri said he would leave it to the authorities to determine if any wrongdoing occurred.
"I have called the person who was detained for an explanation. I was told they did not collect zakat directly, but only assisted with suggestions and processes, including filling out forms," he said.
Asri said that when an NGO receives funds to disburse to schools or for dakwah, a small portion may be allocated to administrators.
"To me, it is not wrong to pay someone for their work. If someone helps secure contributions, and is given three per cent as a token for their effort, that is payment for work, not necessarily corruption.
"The payment comes from the NGO, not MAIPs," he said.
Addressing allegations that zakat funds were invested or used for projects such as a hospital, Asri said justifications had been provided to him, but stressed that the matter should be left to investigators.
"I was informed of certain justifications, but I cannot verify them. Let the authorities investigate," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: 'Tan Sri or Tauke's brother? Doesn't matter- pay for your car wash!' says sign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A notice at a car wash reminding customers to "pay up" once services are rendered has caught the attention of Redditors, with the sign saying that it "doesn't care who you are."
A
photo posted by Reddit
user cheesusfugget shows a notice on a wall written in Mandarin with a Bahasa Malaysia translation alongside it.
"Beginning 2025, it doesn't matter whether you are a Tan Sri, Datuk Seri, Datuk, a cop, a gangster, a best friend, or the tauke's (business owner) brother… Pay up now! Thank you," the notice read.
It added: "Besides God, we only believe in cash."
The post, which garnered nearly 3,000 upvotes, drew a string of responses, many supporting the business's blunt message.
"Honestly based. Business is business, pay up and no drama," wrote one Redditor, Alexisreddit516.
"I don't understand what's going on. Who would want to stiff a car wash place? That sounds like an easy recipe for getting a brick through your window," Felinomancy said.
"Guess the owner already had enough with so-called VIP people who refuse to pay. We don't care about your VIP status whatsoever; just give the money if you're VIP enough," Randomus-08 commented.
Meanwhile, Repulsive-Seesaw-655 wrote: "Can you use QR code payment?"
Pint_Locus said: "I see no problems here."
Signofdacreator commented: "I'm going to save this for the memes."
The location and name of the car wash could not be determined at the time of writing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka police track scammers using department's name to solicit donations
Article:
MELAKA: Police are tracking down individuals who used the force's name to solicit donations from the public through WhatsApp.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said a report was lodged regarding a viral message claiming that the Melaka contingent's Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department was organising an integrity and driving course.
The message alleged that the course, supposedly held at the PDRM Technical College in Cheras, Kuala Lumpur, from today until April 24, required participants or donors to deposit funds into a provided bank account.
Checks confirmed that no such course or programme is being organised by the Melaka traffic department.
The message included photos of uniformed officers and locations within the Melaka police headquarters to appear authentic. It also provided bank account details for the donation collection, he said in a statement today.
Dzulkhairi said the police take the misuse of the department's name seriously as it misleads the public and tarnishes the force's image.
He added that the activity is believed to be a scam intended for illegal profit.
The investigation is being carried out under Section 420/511 of the Penal Code for attempted cheating, which carries a jail term of between one and 10 years, whipping, and a fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lawyer urges fair probe in RM230mil zakat fund case
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The lawyer for a non-governmental organisation (NGO) deputy chairman, who has been remanded over an alleged RM230 million zakat fund misappropriation case, has called for space for investigations to proceed fairly.
Mohd Radzlan Jalaludin said the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigation into his client has so far been conducted properly and professionally.
"We do not want any interference in the investigation. We want it to proceed in a transparent and fair manner, without interference from any party, and without manipulation or sensationalising the case.
"At this stage, my client is only being investigated. There has been no charge, let alone a conviction. We should give MACC the space to carry out its investigation fairly," he told reporters outside the magistrate's court here today.
Earlier, two individuals were remanded for four days to assist in the
investigation
into the alleged misappropriation of about RM230 million in zakat funds.
The remand order was issued by magistrate Ameera Mastura Khamis after allowing MACC's application.
The suspects, aged 57 and 61, are being investigated under Section 16 of the MACC Act 2009.
MACC also seized 18 luxury vehicles, houses and properties worth about RM11 million, as well as other movable assets, including cash and branded watches.
In addition, 33 bank accounts containing about RM120 million have been frozen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KJ: Malaysia could have passed tobacco 'generational endgame' law
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia could have introduced tobacco "generational endgame" (GEG) legislation similar to the United Kingdom's newly approved Tobacco and Vapes Bill if the cabinet had not been wrongly advised, former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin said.
Khairy said he had prepared the legislation before leaving the ministry, but the provision was later removed.
"Congratulations to the UK. Yes, there are legitimate issues around enforcement, but this is a huge win for public health.
"Malaysia could have passed this law, but the provision was dropped after the tobacco and vape industry reportedly convinced the cabinet against it.
"(And) the then attorney-general, in my mind, advised cabinet wrongly," he said in an Instagram story.
His remarks come after both chambers of the United Kingdom's Parliament
approved a bill to ban the sale of cigarettes
to individuals born after Jan 1, 2009 — effectively preventing those currently aged 17 and below from ever legally purchasing tobacco.
The Tobacco and Vapes Bill is aimed at creating a "smoke-free generation", with Health Secretary Wes Streeting describing it as a "historic moment for the nation's health" that would protect future generations from addiction and harm.
On Nov 30, 2023, the Dewan Rakyat passed the
Control of Smoking Products and Public Health Bill
, first proposed in 2022, following intense debate during which many MPs expressed disappointment over the removal of the generational endgame (GEG) provision.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 200 million-litre diesel shipment to Australia not from Malaysia - Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has clarified that Malaysia is not involved in a reported shipment of 200 million litres of diesel allegedly linked to the country and destined for Australia,  after the matter was reported in foreign media reports.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said checks with relevant authorities confirmed that the diesel in question was not Malaysian in origin.
"I read a report from an Australian news agency mentioning 200 million litres of diesel involving several countries including Brunei and Malaysia. However, checks with the MTEN (National Economic Action Council) Communication Command Centre and other parties confirm that it is not Malaysian diesel," he said at the post-Cabinet briefing today.
He explained that confusion may have arisen due to commercial shipping arrangements involving international oil traders operating within Malaysian waters.
"In the case of diesel shipments to the Philippines, for example, there is a trade contract with British Petroleum which has a warehouse in Malaysian waters, and the vessel transporting the diesel departs from Malaysia. But it is not Malaysian diesel," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King extends personal contribution to assist Sandakan fire victims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has extended a personal contribution to assist families affected by a fire in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan.
According to a Facebook post on Sultan Ibrahim's official page, the king decreed that the contribution will be channelled through Yayasan Sultan Ibrahim Johor (YSIJ) to help ease the burden of families impacted by the incident.
Sultan Ibrahim has instructed the foundation to liaise with the Sabah state government to ensure the assistance reaches the victims.
"Many have lost their homes in this tragedy. I pray that the fire victims are granted strength and resilience in facing this hardship," the king said in a statement to the Royal Press Office today.
Last Sunday, a
major fire
at the water village of Kampung Bahagia in Sandakan destroyed about 1,000 houses and affected more than 9,000 people.
The fire involved an area of over four hectares.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan denies RM5.5bil solar project application, approval
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Kelantan state government clarifies that it has neither approved nor received any application related to a proposed RM5.5 billion solar and eco-development project in the state.
In a statement, the state government said the information published in relation to the project is inaccurate and does not involve Kelantan.
"Upon verification by the Economic Planning Unit/State Investment, there has been no approval from the state and no application submitted by the company to the Kelantan state government in relation to the news title mentioned," it said.
The state government is responding to reports regarding a company that had announced plans on April 7 to develop a RM5.5 billion project in northern Kelantan.
The company, Helios, reportedly proposed developing a solar park alongside a wildlife safari conservation and resort project spanning about 1,214 hectares.
The solar park is expected to include a 1,000-megawatt-peak solar photovoltaic facility coupled with a 1,600-megawatt-hour battery energy storage system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thailand backs Myanmar's push to normalise Asean ties
Article:
BANGKOK: Thailand wants to play a leading role in Myanmar's bid to normalise its ties with Southeast Asian bloc Asean after five years on the sidelines, the Thai foreign minister said ahead of a meeting on Wednesday with new President Min Aung Hlaing.
Former military chief Min Aung Hlaing led a 2021 coup that ended a decade of tentative democracy in Myanmar and sparked chaos and a civil war, prompting an intervention by the Association of Southeast Asian Nations and the exclusion of the then ruling generals from its summits.
Min Aung Hlaing, 69, was chosen as president by parliament on April 3, formalising his grip on power after a recent election that was dominated by an army-backed party in the absence of viable opposition.
"We want to support their return to Asean. Our policy is to have steps in our interactions to bring them back," Thai Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow told reporters.
"But certainly, for them to return to Asean, they must be able to proceed and respond to the concerns from Asean."
MYANMAR CRISIS A HEADACHE FOR ASEAN
The conflict in Myanmar has long frustrated Asean, with some members openly critical of the generals for paying only lip service to the bloc's demands for progress on a peace plan that requires all sides to cease hostilities and start dialogue.
Myanmar remains part of Asean has been represented at top-level meetings by ministerial officials.
Few countries have endorsed Myanmar's election or the new military-backed government, with some western nations dismissing the process as a sham designed to entrench the army's control under the guise of civilian rule.
The 11-member Asean bloc has yet to formally recognise the new government. Asean leaders will hold a summit next month in the Philippines.
Sihasak welcomed Myanmar's amnesty last week for thousands of prisoners, which included the release of ousted former President Win Myint and a small sentence reduction for Nobel laureate Aung San Suu Kyi, who was Myanmar's de facto leader at the time of the coup.
"We hope that these kinds of actions will take place more going forward, particularly the reduction of violence," he said.
"Secondly, we know that there is a demand for humanitarian assistance in Myanmar, how they can open a space for Asean or international organisations to go in."
In his inauguration address, Min Aung Hlaing said his priority was to promote peace and reconciliation in Myanmar and he would seek to improve international relations and normalise ties with Asean.
This week he invited opposition armed groups to start dialogue by the end of July, but two key rebel groups rejected the offer on Tuesday. --REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukraine to restart Russian oil pipeline to Europe 'within a few hours'
Article:
KYIV: Ukraine will resume shipping Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia "within a few hours" Wednesday following the completion of repairs to the Druzhba pipeline damaged by a Russian attack, a Kyiv official told AFP.
The pipeline has been at the centre of a standoff between Hungary and Slovakia, which still import Russian oil via the pipeline, and Ukraine and the European Union.
Kyiv hopes the resumption of supplies will unblock the last hurdle to securing tens of billions of euros in support from Brussels that has been held up by Hungary's outgoing nationalist leader Viktor Orban.
Following Ukraine confirming the pipeline had been completed, Hungary's energy giant MOL requested that about 100,000 tonnes of oil be shipped through the pipeline to Hungary and Slovakia, the Ukrainian official said.
Transit was scheduled to resume at around 1100 GMT.
"I think they'll start it earlier, within a few hours," the official told AFP, speaking on condition of anonymity.
Orban had held up a much needed loan of 90 million euros (US$105 million) for Ukraine as leverage to pressure Kyiv to resume deliveries, accusing it of stalling repairs.
His defeat in elections this month was seen as paving the way for the money to be unlocked.
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has made no secret of his opposition to the fact that some EU members still buy Russian oil and gas, a key source of revenue for Moscow to fund its invasion launched more than four years ago.
Talking to reporters in Luxembourg on Tuesday after Kyiv said repairs had been completed, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said she hoped a deal on the loan was within reach.
"We expect an agreement in 24 hours. So I don't want to jinx it. I hope that everything goes well... Hopefully all the obstacles are removed," Kallas said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Govt ramps up cost controls across ministries, agencies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has directed ministries, departments and agencies to step up cost-saving measures, including holding more meetings online, reducing overseas travel and prioritising the use of government premises for official programmes.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the reminder was issued by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during today's Cabinet meeting as part of broader efforts to optimise resource use and manage fuel consumption.
"The Prime Minister reminded the cabinet that, in addition to ministries and departments, agencies under each ministry must also implement priority measures to ensure fuel supplies can be sustained," he said during the weekly post-Cabinet briefing today.
Among the measures proposed are increasing the use of virtual meetings to reduce the need for physical gatherings, scaling down overseas programmes and official travel, and utilising government facilities for workshops and training sessions.
"This includes conducting more meetings online to reduce face-to-face meetings, reducing programmes and overseas travel, and prioritising agency or government premises for workshops and training programmes," Fahmi said.
Fahmi added that the government's work-from-home (WFH) policy for civil servants had so far led to fuel savings of 334,000 litres, worth an estimated RM678,000, since it was started.
"Between 15 April until yesterday, estimated savings amounted to 334,000 litres of fuel, equivalent to RM678,000," Fahmi said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] WFH policy saves 334,000 litres of fuel worth RM678,000 since April 15
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government's work-from-home (WFH) policy for civil servants has led to fuel savings of 334,000 litres, worth an estimated RM678,000, since it was started.
"Between 15 April until yesterday, estimated savings amounted to 334,000 litres of fuel, equivalent to RM678,000," Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said at the weekly post-Cabinet briefing today.
He added that 155,000 civil servants have registered under the WFH programme so far.
The government has also announced wider government efforts to optimise resource use, including directives for ministries and agencies to reduce overseas travel, increase the use of virtual meetings, and prioritise government premises for official training and workshops.
"This includes conducting more meetings online to reduce face-to-face meetings, cutting overseas programmes and travel, and prioritising agency or government premises for workshops and training programmes," Fahmi said.
Fahmi said the Chief Secretary to the Government had also been tasked with obtaining data from the private sector on similar remote working practices to assess potential nationwide savings.
On April 15, the federal public sector began implementing a WFH policy involving civil servants, while public-facing services, particularly counter transactions, continued to operate as usual.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move, covering ministries, agencies, statutory bodies and government-linked companies (GLCs), was aimed at
reducing fuel consumption
and supporting sustainable energy use amid concerns over a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Federal Court to hear Isa Samad's corruption case review in May
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Court will hear on May 13 an application by former Felda chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Samad to review his conviction and sentence in a RM3 million corruption case linked to a hotel acquisition.
Deputy public prosecutor Afzainizam Abdul Aziz and Isa's lawyer, Datuk Abu Bakar Isa Ramat, confirmed the hearing date.
Isa, 77, has been serving a six-year jail sentence at the Sungai Buloh Prison since Feb 10 after the apex court reinstated the imprisonment and a RM15.45 million fine.
The former Negri Sembilan menteri besar was convicted on nine corruption charges involving RM3 million linked to Felda Investment Corporation's acquisition of the Merdeka Palace Hotel & Suites in Kuching, Sarawak.
The top court said the Court of Appeal had erred in overturning the High Court's conviction, finding that the trial judge's decision was supported by evidence and in accordance with the law.
A three-member bench chaired by Datuk Nordin Hassan had said that the prosecution had proven beyond reasonable doubt that Isa had received bribes as a reward for assisting in the approval of the hotel purchase, adding that the statutory presumption under anti-corruption laws had not been rebutted.
On Dec 14, 2018, Isa was charged with one count of criminal breach of trust and nine counts of receiving gratification involving more than RM3 million. The charges related to his approval of a proposal for Felda Investment Corporation Sdn Bhd to purchase the hotel.
The offences were committed on the 49th floor of Menara Felda, Persiaran KLCC, between July 21, 2014, and Dec 11, 2015.
He was charged under Section 16(a)(A) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act, which carries a maximum sentence of 20 years' imprisonment and a fine of not less than five times the bribe amount or RM10,000, whichever is higher.
On March 6, 2024, the Court of Appeal acquitted and discharged Isa of all charges. However, the prosecution appealed the decision, leading to the top court's move to reinstate the conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NASA unveils new space telescope to give 'atlas of the universe'
Article:
WASHINGTON: NASA unveiled a new telescope on Tuesday to scan vast swathes of the universe for planets outside our solar system and probe the mysteries of dark matter and dark energy.
The Roman space telescope is expected to discover tens of thousands of planets, possibly offering clarity about how many could be out there.
"Roman will give the Earth a new atlas of the universe," NASA administrator Jared Isaacman told a news conference at the Goddard Space Flight Center in Maryland, where the telescope went on display.
The 12-metre (39-feet), silvery contraption with massive solar panels will be transported to Florida ahead of a launch into space aboard a SpaceX rocket planned for September at the earliest.
Roman, which took more than US$4 billion and over a decade to build, is named after astronomer Nancy Grace Roman, nicknamed the "Mother of Hubble" for her role in developing the landmark space telescope.
Thirty-six years after Hubble launched into space, revolutionising astronomical observations, NASA hopes Roman will help to shed light on questions that remain unresolved.
Boasting a field of view at least 100 times larger than Hubble's, the telescope will sweep across vast regions of space from its position 1.5 million kilometres (930,000 miles) from Earth.
The telescope will send 11 terabytes of data a day down to Earth, said Mark Melton, a systems engineer at Goddard Space Flight Center.
"In the first year, we'll have sent down more data than Hubble will have for its entire life," he told AFP.
The telescope's wide-angle lens will allow NASA to conduct a census of the objects that make up our universe, said Nicky Fox, associate administrator for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.
"Roman will discover tens of thousands of new planets outside our solar system. It will reveal billions of galaxies, thousands of supernovae and tens of billions of stars," she said.
This wealth of information will enable NASA to tease out areas of interest that can then be investigated by complementary telescopes, such as the James Webb Space Telescope.
But Roman will also study the invisible – dark matter and dark energy, whose origins remain unknown but which are thought to constitute 95 per cent of our universe.
Dark matter is believed to be the glue that holds galaxies together, while dark energy pulls them apart by making the universe expand faster and faster over time.
Thanks to its infrared vision, the telescope will be able to observe light emitted by celestial bodies billions of years ago, effectively looking back in time to hopefully discover more about the two phenomena.
Complementing the work of Europe's Euclid space telescope and the Vera Rubin Observatory in Chile, Roman will probe "how the dark matter structures itself throughout cosmic time" and "calculate how fast galaxies are moving away from us," Darryl Seligman, an assistant professor of physics and astronomy at Michigan State University, told AFP.
These discoveries could fundamentally change our understanding of the structure of our universe, said astrophysicist Julie McEnery, who led the Roman project.
"If Roman wins a Nobel Prize at some point, it's probably for something we haven't even thought about or questioned yet," said Melton.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China cautions of fragile peace as US extends Iran ceasefire
Article:
BEIJING: China warned on Wednesday that the situation in the Middle East was at a "critical juncture" after US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire to allow Iran more time to negotiate.
Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the two-week truce on Tuesday with Teheran yet to respond but he said a US blockade of Iran's ports would continue.
"The current regional situation stands at a critical juncture transitioning between war and peace; the paramount priority remains to make every effort to prevent a resumption of hostilities," Chinese foreign ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun told a news briefing.
Guo did not comment directly on the ceasefire when asked about it, adding only that Beijing would continue to play a "constructive" role.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Erosion fears grow after another Casuarina tree collapses in Batu Ferringhi
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The collapse of another Casuarina tree along the Batu Ferringhi coastline here has heightened safety concerns.
Nearby beach workers have warned of increasing risks linked to coastal erosion and unstable ground conditions.
The latest incident occurred at the beachfront of the Shangri-La Golden Sands Hotel. The hotel has put up signage warning guests about the dangers of erosion and advising them to use designated walking paths.
Beach worker Khairul Izdihar Ismail, 36, said the latest incident occurred about four days ago at 9pm during heavy rain and strong winds.
"I saw the tree starting to give way, but fortunately no one was in the area at the time," he told the New Straits Times when met at the beach today.
Khairul said the tree fell onto sandbags placed along the embankment, which he claimed had already been weakened due to ongoing soil erosion and insufficient sand support.
"The shoreline is eroding and there isn't enough sand to stabilise the ground. That's why the trees are becoming unsafe," he said.
Khairul added that the situation worsened the following day when part of a stone panel structure along the embankment collapsed.
"A friend of mine was injured when the structure gave way. He suffered multiple injuries," he said, adding that the victim has not returned to work since the incident.
Khairul also pointed to a similar incident about a month ago near the Lone Pine Hotel, and claimed that since February, at least eight large Casuarina trees have collapsed along the stretch.
Earlier this year, the Penang government acknowledged severe erosion on the Tanjung Bungah and Batu Ferringhi beaches.
State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the erosion, while a recurring natural phenomenon during the northeast monsoon, was significantly more severe compared with previous years, particularly along the Batu Ferringhi coastline.
The federal government has approved a RM61 million project under the 12th Malaysia Plan (Rolling Plan 5), involving the
construction
of outfalls and coastal erosion prevention structures in Batu Ferringhi.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor police seek help to locate missing Rohingya girl, woman
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor police are seeking public assistance to locate two missing persons reported in separate cases in the state.
A 14-year-old Rohingya girl, identified as Nurqayas Noor Mohamad, was reported missing by her father on March 30 from their home in Taman Nusantara, Iskandar Puteri.
She is about 150cm tall and weighs 50kg. She was last seen at No. 8, Jalan Nusaria 3/4, Taman Nusantara, Gelang Patah.
Those with information are urged to contact investigating officer Sgt Nor Syafiqa Hamzon at 013-7416882 or the district control centre at 07-5113622.
In a separate case, Johor Baru North police are appealing for public assistance to trace a 42-year-old woman, who has been missing since April 14.
The woman, Xie Lixia, was last seen at her residence in Taman Sutera about 4.45pm on April 13. She was reportedly wearing a brown T-shirt and jeans at the time.
She is 160cm tall, weighs 57kg, straight long black hair and a slim build.
Members of the public are urged to channel any information on Xie's whereabouts to the police hotline at 07-5561222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt WFH in its first week saves 334,000 litres of fuel and RM678,000, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Malaysia’s work-from-home push for civil servants has saved about RM678,000 in fuel in just over a week, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today.
He said the initiative, which began on April 15, has already involved some 155,000 civil servants nationwide.
“Since April 25, he said the initiative has resulted in an estimated fuel saving of 334,000 litres, equivalent to approximately RM678,000,” he said.
Fahmi said the Cabinet was briefed on the programme’s progress by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
He added that the chief secretary has been tasked with gathering similar data from the private sector to gauge potential nationwide savings if flexible work arrangements are expanded.
Separately, Fahmi said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission has received 463 requests from the public to remove false online content linked to the global supply crisis.
“All requests would be forwarded to the relevant social media platforms for further action,” he said.
“As of 7am today, 463 requests have been received with Facebook accounting for the highest number with 185 requests, followed by TikTok with 142 and Threads with 131,” he added.
Fahmi said 65 investigation papers have been opened so far, with one case already referred to the Attorney General’s Chambers for prosecution.
He added that five cases have resulted in compound notices, while others are still under investigation.
He said most of the false content involved fuel prices and claims related to a supposed Strait of Hormuz toll.

Title: One night, one month: Syed Saddiq says fiancée Bella Astillah out-earns his MP salary per show
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has revealed that his fiancée, singer-actress Bella Astillah, can earn in a single performance what he makes in a month as a politician,
Berita Harian
reported today.
Speaking in a podcast interview, he said the comparison highlights the stark differences in income structures between the entertainment industry and politics.
“People think politicians are highly paid. In reality, earnings outside are better.
“I told my fiancée that if she performs for one night, that’s already equivalent to my monthly salary,” he said.
He added that he himself earns significantly more when taking on filming work.
“If I spend a day filming, the pay can match two to three months of my salary as a Member of Parliament.
“And it’s just a day’s work, without the pressure, without losing privacy, and without constant attacks,” he said.
Addressing speculation that he might stop Bella from continuing her career after marriage, Syed Saddiq dismissed the idea.
“I don’t agree with the view that Bella shouldn’t act, sing or appear in public except for official events after this.
“That’s not fair. Because of me, her dreams should not be sacrificed. Let her continue pursuing her ambitions,” he said.
However, he admitted his main concern is that his outspoken political stance could affect Bella’s opportunities in the entertainment industry.
“I’m more worried that my position in politics might impact her career. That’s what concerns me,” he said.
He also denied claims that he intends to bring Bella into politics.
“Some say this is a way to get Bella into politics. No. She can teach me how to dance, but she doesn’t need to enter politics.
“I don’t want her to go through what I’ve gone through,” he said.
Instead, he said he encourages her to take part in healthy activities such as sports.
In the same interview, Syed Saddiq shared that he is currently in a happy phase of his relationship with Bella, but is not yet ready to disclose their wedding date.
The couple got engaged on March 28 and are expected to tie the knot in the near future.

Title: KLIA baggage fiasco: MAHB to boost manpower, submit weekly reports after Cabinet review
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB) has been directed to take urgent measures, including boosting manpower at critical passenger areas, following a major baggage handling disruption at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) last weekend.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was raised by Transport Minister Anthony Loke during today’s Cabinet meeting, where concerns were voiced over the operational failure.
“MAHB will carry out a number of immediate steps, including increasing the number of personnel in arrival areas and other critical locations that have been identified, as well as submitting weekly progress reports to the Ministry of Transport for Cabinet updates,” he said during an online press conference today.
The disruption on Saturday, which lasted approximately five hours, was traced to a high-voltage fault at a Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) substation in Bukit Raja.
Preliminary findings showed a trip in a 132kV Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) unit at about 4:54 PM triggered a voltage dip, crippling operations at KLIA Terminal 1 until services were fully restored around 10:30 PM.
During the five-hour disruption, a total of 23,769 bags were processed.
However, the system failure resulted in 1,061 bags being short-shipped (left behind) and 120 bags recorded as missing upon arrival, causing significant inconvenience to travellers.

Title: Takeovers not a crime, SC says amid ‘corporate mafia’ allegations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Securities Commission Malaysia (SC) today said it has no authority to act on allegations of a “corporate mafia” if the issues fall outside securities laws.
Chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said the regulator’s role is limited to market and securities offences, not criminal conduct such as intimidation or extortion, business news outlet The Edge reported.
“Why do you think that’s my job? My job is securities laws,” he was quoted as saying in a media conference after releasing the SC’s 2025 annual report here.
Faiz said that corporate activities like share purchases or takeover bids are legal as long as they comply with established takeover regulations.
He said public perception of wrongdoing does not automatically mean an offence under securities law has been committed.
He also noted that reports involving threats or coercion are matters for the police, not the market regulator.
“People are open to buying and selling their shares, and it’s not a crime to want to take over a company, provided they follow our takeover rules,” Faiz was quoted as saying.
He said that any alleged intimidation or harassment linked to corporate activity should be investigated by enforcement agencies with criminal jurisdiction.
“When intimidation and harassment is involved, these are matters really for the police to investigate, because they have wider powers and stronger penalties,” he added.
Faiz also said SC enforcement must be backed by evidence and proper investigation, not media speculation or complaints alone.
He said cases require detailed inquiry, including witness interviews and data analysis, before any action can be taken.
Allegations of a “corporate mafia” culture emerged after Bloomberg reported on the dubious share sales and company takeovers involving connected individuals and officials
These allegations have been denied by the MACC and its chief commissioner.
Faiz said regulatory action must also balance enforcement with fairness to avoid harming companies based on unproven claims.
He added that the SC is strengthening enforcement capabilities through improved technology and cooperation with other agencies, including Bank Negara Malaysia and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission.

Title: Tuanku Muhriz to officiate Negri Sembilan assembly sitting tomorrow amid Undang controversy
Article:
SEREMBAN, April 22 — The Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, is due to officiate the opening of the State Legislative Assembly tomorrow, with the ceremony proceeding as planned despite ongoing scrutiny of the state’s royal institution.
State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim confirmed today that the opening will take place at 10 am, with Tuanku Muhriz inspecting the guard of honour and delivering the royal address,
Berita Harian
reported.
“Tomorrow’s DUN opening ceremony will proceed as scheduled. The Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz, is scheduled to grace the occasion to officiate at the ceremony,” Mohd Zafir told reporters.
“It will proceed as normal.”
When asked to comment on preparations in light of the reported crisis between the Four Undangs and the Yang di-Pertuan Besar, Mohd Zafir could not confirm the attendance of other customary dignitaries, including the district chiefs.
“As for the (VVIP) invitations, I am unable to elaborate, but Tuanku will be present to officiate,” he said, confirming that official invitation letters had been sent out.
The ceremony is customarily attended by the Four Undangs — Datuk Mubarak Dohak (Sungei Ujong), Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad (Jelebu), Datuk Muhammad Abdullah (Johol), and Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin (Rembau) — as well as the Tunku Besar Tampin, Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al Qadri.
According to protocol, the Tunku Besar Tampin and the Four Undangs are scheduled to enter the assembly chamber before the arrival of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
The situation in Negeri Sembilan has drawn public attention following reports of a move by the district chiefs (undang luak) to use their powers to remove Tuanku Muhriz as the state’s ruler.
There are also separate allegations that the Undang of Sungei Ujong has been dismissed from his position.
Despite the situation, preparations for the ceremony are continuing, with a full rehearsal of the guard of honour already conducted.
The State Assembly sitting is scheduled to resume from April 27 to 29 following tomorrow’s opening.

Title: Selangor PAS chief labels Shah Alam floods a ‘planning failure’, demands action plan
Article:
SHAH ALAM, April 22 — The Selangor government has been urged to increase green space allocation in Shah Alam residential areas to 30 per cent and review its development policies, with the state’s PAS chief labelling the recurring floods a “planning failure”.
Paya Jaras assemblyman Datuk Ab. Halim Tamuri, debating the Royal Address at the Selangor State Legislative Assembly today, argued that the state capital’s flood woes reflect ingrained weaknesses in planning and management.
He questioned if the state government considers that it has failed to uphold the original planning principles of Shah Alam, which was designed as a model township with a focus on environmental balance.
“The failure to address flooding in Shah Alam is no longer a simple matter,” Halim said.
“Does the state government acknowledge that the recurring floods, occurring day after day, whether yesterday, today, or before, are the result of a failure to uphold those planning principles?”
The Selangor PAS chief also criticised the lack of consistent maintenance of drains and waterways, which he said has led to drainage systems failing even during normal rainfall.
He demanded a clear and measurable action plan from the state government within the next six to 12 months, complete with key performance indicators (KPIs) and clear lines of accountability.
“Who will be held responsible if this issue persists?” he asked.
As a long-term solution, Halim proposed that the green space allocation in Shah Alam’s residential areas be tripled from the current estimated 10 per cent to 30 per cent.
“I would like to propose that the development policy for residential areas be reviewed, particularly in ensuring sufficient green spaces,” he said.
“This is one of the approaches to better manage the environment and reduce the impact of flooding.”
The debate follows a sharp rebuke from Selangor Ruler Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah on Monday.
During the opening of the state assembly, the Sultan criticised the state government for its failure to effectively address the persistent flooding despite his repeated advice.
A day later, State Infrastructure and Agriculture Exco Datuk Izham Hashim responded by saying that the state’s flood woes are unlikely to be fully resolved for another four years, as major flood mitigation projects are still ongoing.

Title: Duo fined RM6,000 for confining Indonesian migrants, withholding passports
Article:
BATU PAHAT, April 22 — A 71-year-old man and a 43-year-old Indonesian single mother have each been fined RM3,000 after pleading guilty to unlawfully confining two Indonesian migrants and illegally retaining their passports.
Ker Peng Hoe and Hartati Agostoni were charged before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass under Section 340 of the Penal Code, punishable under Section 342 read together with Section 34 of the same code,
Berita Harian
reported.
The pair were accused of wrongfully confining two Indonesian women at a shophouse in Taman Flora Utama here between 9 am and 8.30 pm on April 19.
On the second charge, both were accused of possessing the victims’ passports, with the offences taking place at the Batu Pahat District Police Headquarters and at a room in Taman Flora Utama between 3.30 and 4.30 pm on April 20, under Section 12(1)(f) of the Passport Act 1966, which carries a fine of up to RM10,000 or imprisonment of up to five years.
Ker, a father of five, and Hartati, a mother of two, were each fined RM2,000 on the first charge and RM1,000 on the second.
The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan, while the accused were represented by lawyers Tan Song Yan and Tan Fong Ling.

Title: Khaled Nordin: Desalination plant, cloud seeding deployed as water crisis hits 4,000 in Kota Tinggi
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, April 22 — Authorities are deploying a mobile desalination plant and conducting cloud seeding operations to combat a severe water crisis affecting more than 4,000 residents in Felda Lok Heng and Felda Waha.
According to Kota Tinggi MP Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin, prolonged dry weather has caused water levels at Sungai Ulu Sedili Kecil to drop to a critical point, rendering the Lok Heng Water Treatment Plant unable to operate.
In a statement today, Khaled, who is also the defence minster, explained that previous mitigation efforts, including using groundwater from tube wells and transferring water from Sungai Lukah, had proven unsustainable due to limited resources and high salinity levels.
To address the crisis, water operator Ranhill SAJ is now relocating and installing a desalination plant from Forest City to the Lok Heng facility.
The unit is expected to produce up to two million litres of treated water daily, though Khaled noted its implementation is facing a slight delay.
“It is hoped that this effort will help restore and restabilise the water supply as soon as possible, thereby reducing the hardship faced by affected residents,” he said.
In addition to the desalination plant, cloud seeding operations are scheduled for today and tomorrow, April 22 and 23.
Khaled said that these dates were chosen because atmospheric conditions are now more favourable for the procedure, after earlier attempts were hindered by unsuitable cloud cover.
Khaled also urged residents to remain patient and offered a prayer for a swift resolution to the water supply challenge.

Title: Malaysia’s strategic neutrality key to navigating US-China trade war, says Tengku Zafrul
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Malaysia should reform or recalibrate its response to changes in the global architecture, especially in trade, as they occur, said Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz.
He said the country should also remain mindful of its strategic positioning as a middle power, leveraging its strengths while addressing concerns about energy security, technology, and overall resilience to remain part of both the United States and China’s supply chain ecosystems.
“For MIDA and Malaysia, we know our strengths, but at the same time, we must be ready for challenges ahead,” he said during a panel session at the CNBC Converge Live programme titled ‘Redrawing The Global Trade Map’, hosted by its Morning Call anchor, Morgan Brennan, today.
He said Malaysia needs to continue engaging with the US and China as the two economies account for nearly 30 per cent of the country’s total trade.
Tengku Zafrul further noted that while China remains Malaysia’s and ASEAN’s largest trading partner, the US is Malaysia’s largest investor and export market and possibly for the broader region as well.
“We have been consistent and will engage with both countries, as we are talking about supply chain security and technology security, and they want to have trusted supply chain partners.
“So Malaysia and ASEAN can play that role (as trusted supply chain partners), given our position when we talk about strategic neutrality,” he said.
As an open economy, Tengku Zafrul noted that Malaysia’s strengths in the semiconductor industry, digital economy and energy sector, supported by consistent policies, enabled the country to remain neutral and relevant within global supply chains.
“Things are dynamic. People are watching closely to see how we (Malaysia) navigate the current situation in West Asia.
“I hope that with consistent policies and strategic key decisions being made to look at the longer term, as this will benefit Malaysia and the region as well,” he said.
On energy security amid the ongoing West Asia crisis, Tengku Zafrul said Malaysia is currently in a relatively strong position as a net energy exporter, which places the country on a firm economic footing.
He added that Malaysia’s capital markets remain optimistic, recording a more than 10 per cent year-on-year increase, supported by positive gross domestic product (GDP) growth, investment performance, stable unemployment, and policy flexibility on both fiscal and monetary fronts.
“But we can’t take these things for granted. In my view, we are seeing a reallocation of assets by companies and fund managers in capital markets and in foreign investment for the longer term,” he added. — Bernama

Title: May 13 hearing for Isa Samad’s bid to review prison term, RM15.45m fine
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Former Felda chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad’s application to review the Federal Court’s decision on his conviction and sentence will be heard on May 13.
Mohd Isa, who filed the application on March 4, is seeking a review of the six-year jail sentence and RM15.45 million fine imposed on him over corruption charges.
Lawyer Datuk Abu Bakar Isa Ramat, representing Mohd Isa, and Deputy Public Prosecutor Afzainizam Abdul Aziz confirmed the hearing date when contacted. Mohd Isa is currently serving his sentence at Sungai Buloh Prison.
On Feb 10, the Federal Court reinstated his conviction and six-year jail sentence, along with a fine of RM15.45 million, or an additional two years’ imprisonment in default.
A three-judge panel led by Datuk Nordin Hassan allowed the prosecution’s appeal, overturning the Court of Appeal’s 2024 decision, which had acquitted Mohd Isa of nine corruption charges.
The panel reinstated the High Court’s Feb 3, 2021 decision sentencing him to six years’ imprisonment and a RM15.45 million fine, or two years’ jail in default.
The former Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar was charged with receiving RM3.09 million in bribes from then Gegasan Abadi Properties Sdn Bhd (GAPSB) board member Ikhwan Zaidel, through his former special officer Muhammad Zahid Md Arip.
The payment was allegedly an inducement to approve Felda Investment Corporation Sdn Bhd’s (FICSB) purchase of the Merdeka Palace Hotel & Suites in Kuching, Sarawak, for RM160 million.
In the unanimous decision, Justice Nordin said the High Court had correctly found that Mohd Isa received the bribes, triggering the presumption under Section 50(1) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2009.
The provision states that any gratification received, given or solicited is presumed to be corrupt unless proven otherwise.
Justice Nordin said that while conveying ‘salam’ (greeting) through an intermediary is a common practice among Muslims, in this case, it carried a different connotation, and the respondent’s communication to Muhammad Zahid, together with the ‘salam’, supported evidence of a request or demand for the bribe. — Bernama

Title: Zakat Selangor says has absolutely no links to RM230m NGO fraud case
Article:
SHAH ALAM, April 22 — Lembaga Zakat Selangor (Zakat Selangor) has stressed that it has no connection to the alleged misappropriation of
zakat
funds amounting to RM230 million, which is currently under investigation by the authorities.
Zakat Selangor chief executive officer Mohd Khaidzir Shahari said the institution operates as Selangor’s official zakat authority under the supervision of the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (MAIS), and is governed by legal provisions as well as a comprehensive governance and regulatory framework with checks and balances.
“Zakat fund management also undergoes multiple layers of audit and monitoring, including internal and external audits, as well as reviews by the authorities, to ensure that all zakat funds are managed in an orderly and controlled manner.
“Zakat Selangor has taken note of media reports on the arrest of several individuals in connection with the alleged misappropriation of zakat funds amounting to about RM230 million currently under investigation, and stresses that the case involves a non-governmental organisation (NGO) and has no connection with Zakat Selangor or the management of zakat by the institution,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Khaidzir said Zakat Selangor maintained a firm stance against any form of misuse of zakat funds and would always ensure that zakat management was carried out in accordance with the established governance framework.
He added that the institution would not issue further comments as the investigation is still ongoing, and advised the public to refer to its official channels for accurate information and to make zakat payments through www.zakatselangor.com.my.
At the same time, Mohd Khaidzir reminded all parties not to spread false information on the matter, as it could cause confusion and undermine public confidence in the zakat institution. — Bernama

Title: MACC clarifies that RM230m misappropriated was public donations, not ‘zakat’ funds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has clarified that the alleged misappropriation of RM230 million by a non-governmental organisation (NGO) did not involve funds from any official
zakat
institution, but rather public donations intended for the needy.
MACC Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki stated that a portion of the misappropriated funds is also believed to include collections that should have been channelled to a state
zakat
body but never were,
Sinar Harian
reported.
The clarification comes after two individuals, including the NGO’s deputy chairman, were remanded for four days to assist in the high-profile investigation.
In a statement on Wednesday, Azam Baki explained that the case does not implicate any of the country’s formal
zakat
institutions.
“In MACC's latest investigation, this NGO received donation funds from various parties and sources, including commissions obtained from certain collections, to be channelled to those in need,” he said.
“However, these funds did not reach their intended recipients. Instead, there were those among them who irresponsibly misappropriated these funds for personal gain, including the purchase of luxury assets.”
He confirmed that the investigation is ongoing and its scope is being expanded.
Preliminary investigations suggest that the suspects, two of whom are also company directors, colluded to commit the offences between 2018 and 2024.
It is believed the NGO’s deputy chairman transferred the RM230 million into his own company’s account for investment purposes and personal use.
The MACC has since frozen 33 bank accounts containing approximately RM120 million.
In a large-scale operation, the commission also seized assets worth millions, including 18 luxury vehicles, with Porsche and Mercedes models among them; houses and properties valued at around RM11 million; and  various other assets, including cash and branded watches.
The two male suspects, aged in their 50s and 60s, were arrested on Tuesday in an operation around the Klang Valley.
A third individual was also arrested but has not been remanded.
